# M1.5 — Shared Context (Launch Readiness)

This file is the source of truth for any session working on M1.5. It captures
exactly what already exists, the app's identity and bundle metadata, and the
non-negotiable ethical commitments that govern every store-facing string.

> **IMPORTANT — read first**: M1.5's backlog node was authored when Isar was the
> assumed persistence layer for stats. Reality: the codebase ships a working
> SharedPreferences-backed `AnalyticsService` plus a fully functional
> [analytics_screen.dart](../../lib/screens/analytics_screen.dart). E1.5.1 is
> effectively complete — do **not** reimplement it in Isar or under
> `lib/features/stats/` unless the user explicitly asks for the migration.

## App Metadata (verified)

| Item | Value | Source |
|------|-------|--------|
| Display name | RunThru | pubspec.yaml `name: runthru` |
| Tagline | "Your attention isn't broken. It just needs a better direction." | CLAUDE.md |
| Positioning | Paced reading for completion (NOT speed reading) | CLAUDE.md |
| Version | `2.0.0+15` | pubspec.yaml |
| Android applicationId | `com.runthru.app` | android/app/build.gradle |
| iOS bundle id | `com.mgmacri.runthru` | ios/Runner.xcodeproj |
| iOS Share Ext bundle id | `com.mgmacri.runthru.ShareExtension` | ios/ShareExtension |
| Min iOS | 16+ | doc/speedy-boy-design-spec-v3.md |
| Min Android | API 28+ | doc/speedy-boy-design-spec-v3.md |

⚠ **Bundle ID mismatch is intentional** — Android uses `com.runthru.app`, iOS
uses `com.mgmacri.runthru`. Don't "fix" one to match the other without asking.

## Existing Analytics — DO NOT REIMPLEMENT

Stats are already wired end-to-end. Verbatim signatures from the codebase:

### `lib/store/analytics_models.dart`

```dart
class ReadingSession {
  const ReadingSession({
    required this.startTime,
    this.endTime,
    required this.wordsRead,
    required this.avgWpm,
    required this.filePath,
  });
  factory ReadingSession.fromJson(Map<String, Object?> json);
  final DateTime startTime;
  final DateTime? endTime;
  final int wordsRead;
  final double avgWpm;
  final String filePath;
  Duration? get duration;
  Map<String, Object?> toJson();
}

class DailyWpm {
  const DailyWpm({required this.date, required this.avgWpm, required this.sessionsCount});
  final DateTime date;
  final double avgWpm;
  final int sessionsCount;
}

class ReadingStats {
  const ReadingStats({
    this.totalSessions = 0,
    this.totalWordsRead = 0,
    this.avgWpm = 0.0,
    this.streak = 0,
    this.wpmHistory = const [],
  });
  final int totalSessions;
  final int totalWordsRead;
  final double avgWpm;
  final int streak;
  final List<DailyWpm> wpmHistory;
}
```

### `lib/services/analytics_service.dart`

```dart
class AnalyticsService {
  Future<List<ReadingSession>> getSessions();
  Future<void> saveSession(ReadingSession session); // skips wordsRead <= 0; capped at 1000
  Future<ReadingStats> calculateStats();
  Future<void> clearAllData();
}

final analyticsServiceProvider = Provider<AnalyticsService>((ref) => AnalyticsService());
```

Persistence: SharedPreferences key `reading_sessions`, JSON-encoded list, capped
at 1000 entries (oldest dropped). All on-device. No network.

### Session lifecycle (already implemented)

- [reading_screen.dart](../../lib/screens/reading_screen.dart) tracks
  `_sessionStart` and `_sessionWordCount`. Calls `saveSession()` on pause,
  dispose, and document change.
- [parallax_reading_screen.dart](../../lib/screens/parallax_reading_screen.dart)
  mirrors the same pattern.
- "Completion" is implicit: any session that ended with `wordsRead > 0` is
  persisted.

### Analytics screen (already rendering)

[analytics_screen.dart](../../lib/screens/analytics_screen.dart) is a
`ConsumerStatefulWidget` that loads `ReadingStats` in `initState`, renders an
`fl_chart` WPM line, stat cards, streak badge, and an empty state. Uses
`RunThruTokens` colors and `RunThruTypography` text from
`package:runthru/design/design.dart`.

## Existing Fastlane (already wired)

### `android/fastlane/Fastfile`

- `lane :beta` → `build_android_app` (bundle/Release) → `upload_to_play_store(track: "internal")`
- `lane :release` → promote internal → production via `upload_to_play_store`

### `ios/fastlane/Fastfile`

- `lane :beta` → `build_app(workspace: "Runner.xcworkspace", scheme: "Runner")` → `upload_to_testflight(skip_waiting_for_build_processing: true)`
- `lane :release` → `build_app` → `upload_to_app_store(skip_metadata: true, skip_screenshots: true)`

## Ethical Commitments (NON-NEGOTIABLE — apply to ALL store strings)

These are blocker-level rules. Any violation must be flagged, not silently
fixed-and-shipped.

1. **No ableist language**, anywhere. Banned phrases include but are not limited to:
   - "suffering from ADHD" / "suffering from dyslexia"
   - "overcoming a disability"
   - "normal readers" / "normal reading"
   - "fix your attention"
   - "broken brain" / "your brain is broken"
   - "cure" / "treatment" framing for any neurotype
2. **Positioning is completion, not speed.** Do not lead with "read faster",
   WPM numbers, or competitive framing. Lead with "finish what you start",
   "get through what you need to read", or "your attention isn't broken — it
   just needs a better direction."
3. **No dark patterns.** No fake urgency, fake trial countdowns, no "people
   are reading right now" social proof, no shame-on-cancel flows.
4. **Privacy by default.** All reading content stays on device. The privacy
   policy must reflect this literally — no weasel words like "we may collect".
5. **Accessibility features are NEVER paywalled.** OpenDyslexic, adaptive
   spacing, reading ruler, CVD themes, font size — all free, forever. The
   store description and any screenshots must not imply otherwise.
6. **No cloud upload of reading content.** If/when sync is added, it must be
   opt-in per session.

## Hard Rules (subset relevant to M1.5)

- All copy in `doc/store-listing/` is plain Markdown — no HTML, no Liquid.
- Privacy policy is plain language. If a sentence requires legal training to
  parse, rewrite it.
- Fastlane secrets (signing keys, API keys, App Store Connect API) live in
  environment variables only. Never commit a key, never inline a token.
- ASO keyword density target: 2–4% per primary keyword. Don't keyword-stuff.
- No raw `Color(0xFF...)` if any new UI is added — use design tokens.

## Out of Scope — Do NOT Touch

- `lib/core/orp.dart` — pacing engine is the competitive moat
- `lib/three_d/` — 3D viewport
- `lib/services/analytics_service.dart` (already done; don't migrate to Isar)
- `lib/store/analytics_models.dart` (already done)
- Any reading mode widget — M1.4 is shipped
- Any Instapaper file — E1.3.5 is shipped

## Test / Build Baseline

- `dart analyze --fatal-infos` → zero issues
- `flutter test` → no new failures vs baseline (453 pass / 0 skip / 4 pre-existing)
- `flutter build apk --release` → succeeds
- `flutter build ios --no-codesign` → succeeds

## File Layout for M1.5 Deliverables

```
doc/
  store-listing/
    app_store_description.md          # ≤4000 chars, ASO-tuned
    play_store_short_description.md   # ≤80 chars
    play_store_full_description.md    # ≤4000 chars
    keywords.md                       # primary + secondary keyword list, density notes
    screenshots/                      # PNG exports (optional in scope)
  legal/
    privacy_policy.md                 # plain-language, GDPR + CCPA compliant
android/fastlane/                     # already exists — augment, don't rewrite
ios/fastlane/                         # already exists — augment, don't rewrite
```
